name: Chorus/Flange
description: This algorithm is a stereo chorus or flanger effect. For a chorus effect, select a longish Delay with a low Depth and low Feedback. For a flanger, you want a short Delay and a high Depth, and add feedback to taste. The Spread control sets the phase difference in the LFOs for the left and right channel. When at the default setting of 180 you get the 'stereo chorus' effect provided on certain classic synths to generate a stereo output from an essentially mono source.

parameters.name: Output mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector as described above.

parameters.name: Delay
parameters.description: The size of the delay lines.

parameters.name: Speed
parameters.description: The LFO speed; exponential scaling from 0.01Hz to 20Hz.

parameters.name: Spread
parameters.description: The LFO phase difference between channels.

parameters.name: Level
parameters.description: The gain applied to the wet signal.

input_ports.name: Left/mono input
input_ports.description: The left or mono input bus.

input_ports.name: Right input
input_ports.description: The right input bus, if stereo.

input_ports.name: Left/mono input
input_ports.description: The left or mono input bus.

input_ports.name: Right input
input_ports.description: The right input bus, if stereo.

output_ports.name: Right output
output_ports.description: The right output bus, if stereo.

output_ports.name: Right output
output_ports.description: The right output bus, if stereo.
